content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants                Date : May 16, 1978

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Sarah T. Cameron

Subject: Lobbyist Employers Whose Financial Interests are not Clear
         From Their Reports


         Attached is a listing of lobbyist employers who I
think might be candidates for reporting organizations
which donate large amounts of money under some program
similar to the federal one which requires a list of
organizations donating $3,000 or more to another
organization which spends more than 1% of its annual
income on lobbying. The numbers refer to the part of
Schedule C of Form 650 where they identified themselves.
There were eight other employers in the 1977 book which
I wanted to check out but whose files were not in the
file cabinet at the Secretary of State's Office.


STC:nc

Attachment

a.  Committee on Moral Concerns

    Part 4A - "Committee of pastors and church members of
various denominations providing information to members con-
cerning moral issues, trends and laws."

    4B - "church members of various denominations"


b.  California Pro Life Council

    Part 4A - "CPLC is a civic organization working to rid
society of abortion, euthansia and other assorted evils."

    4B - blank


c.  California Taxpayer's Association

    Part 4A - "Promote economy in government through research,
appearances before legislative bodies and support for local
taxpayer groups.  Cal-Tax works to control expenditures at
all levels."

    4B - "Over 1,000 firms representing all segments
of industry, commerce and agriculture, as well as individual
members and local taxpayer organizations."


d.  Californians for Right to Work

    Part 4A - "Conduct a statewide education program designed
to bring about the understanding of the right to work
principle.  Work to obtain legislation which will protect all
workers - public and private - from compulsory unionism and
curb the use of compulsory union dues for political activity."

    4B - "None"


e.  California Common Cause

    Part 4A - "Non-profit, non-partisan, public interest
citizens' lobbying organization."

    4B - blank


f.  California Council for Environmental & Economic Balance

    Part 3A - "The California Council for Environmental &
Economic Balance offers programs, coordinates efforts and seeks
wide support for improving the environment while maintaining
healthy economic growth."

Page Two

f. (continued)
                    3B - "Statewide, hence - not applicable."

                    3C - more than 50 members

g. People's Lobby

        Part 4A - "Non-profit organization, charitable Trust -
political, environmental and social reform,initiative education;
judicial action; legislative action; publication
                    4B - blank

h. Planning & Conservation League

        Part 4A - "Planning & conservation organization."

                    4B - "General public"

i. Women in Timber

        Part 3A - "An organization of women related to the timber
industry and involved in legislative and public relations pro-
grams."

                    3B - blank

                    3C - over 50

j. A Better Citizen Foundation, Inc.

        Part 2A - "Educational Court Referral Program."

k. Let The People Be Heard Committee (John Henry Leach II)

        Part 1A - "Let The People Be Heard Committee" [no address]

                1B - "Enacting of Legislative and Congressional
Legislation"

                Part 2 - "Political Consultant"

sections.question_synthetic: Which lobbyist employers may qualify as reporting organizations under a proposed disclosure program similar to federal requirements for organizations donating $3,000 or more to entities that spend over 1% of annual income on lobbying?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal memorandum, not an FPPC advice letter responding to an external request. No formal question or conclusion sections present. Document appears to be a preliminary analysis or staff work product identifying organizations for potential reporting requirements rather than providing legal advice. Some OCR errors present in original document (e.g., 'demoninations', 'Coromi ttee').

embedding.summary: This is an internal FPPC memorandum from Sarah T. Cameron to opinion request meeting participants listing ten lobbyist employers whose financial interests lack clarity in their reports, with descriptions of their stated purposes and organizational structures from Schedule C filings.
